core_mechanism: Computation of an input-to-output scattering matrix (S-matrix) for interacting quantized excitations via Lehmann-Symanzik-Zimmermann reduction — extracting asymptotic in-to-out transition amplitudes from the on-shell poles/residues of time-ordered field correlation functions, and identifying bound states as sub-threshold poles of the same propagator.
brief_reason: The abstract explicitly builds its whole method on the LSZ reduction to obtain multi-photon scattering S-matrices and to locate single-excitation bound states, which is precisely the machinery of reducing scattering and bound-state amplitudes to asymptotic limits of a field theory's correlation functions.
